tokens.title: Token Usage
tokens.subtitle: Configure AI token limits and alerts
tokens.demoModeMessage: Showing simulated token usage data. To see live token consumption from your AI operations, ensure the kc-agent is running and connected.

validation.warningMustBeLower: Warning threshold must be lower than critical threshold.
validation.limitZeroConfirm: Setting limit to 0 disables all AI operations. Continue?

github.title: GitHub Integration
github.subtitle: Configure GitHub API access for all GitHub features
github.classicInstructions: Check the 'repo' scope. This covers issue creation, screenshot uploads, and all other GitHub features. Works with any public repo — no org membership required.
github.fineGrainedInstructions: Only for self-hosted deployments where you control the target repo. Grant 'Issues: Read and write' and 'Contents: Read and write'. Fine-grained tokens cannot access org repos like kubestellar/console unless the org explicitly allows them.
github.securityWarning: Token is stored in encrypted server-side settings. Use a token with minimal permissions and set an expiration date.
github.feedbackTokenDescription: Used for all GitHub operations: bug/feature submission, screenshot uploads, activity card, CI monitoring, and missions. Classic PAT: 'repo' scope. Fine-grained PAT: 'Issues' + 'Contents' read/write.
github.feedbackTokenNotConfiguredHint: GitHub features are limited (60 requests/hour, no issue submission)
github.feedbackTokenSetupHint: To enable full GitHub integration, add a GitHub PAT here or set FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN in your .env file. Classic PAT: 'repo' scope. Fine-grained PAT: 'Issues' + 'Contents' read/write.
github.clearConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove the GitHub token? GitHub features will be limited until a new token is configured.

predictions.title: Predictive Failure Detection
predictions.subtitle: Configure AI-powered prediction settings
predictions.aiPredictionsDesc: Analyze cluster data with AI to detect patterns
predictions.analysisIntervalDesc: How often AI analyzes cluster data. Lower = more API calls.
predictions.minConfidenceDesc: Only show predictions with confidence above this threshold.
predictions.multiProviderDesc: Run analysis on multiple AI providers, boost confidence when they agree
predictions.heuristicThresholdsDesc: These thresholds trigger instant predictions without AI analysis.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Update your contact information
profile.affiliateSubtitle: Share this link to earn credits on the contributor leaderboard
profile.affiliateChannelHint: Pick the channel you're sharing on — helps us attribute traffic
profile.affiliateLoginMissing: Sign in with GitHub to generate your affiliate link.
profile.affiliateFootnote: Counts update within 24–48h (Google Analytics attribution lag). Chat apps like WhatsApp and Discord strip query strings from link previews — prefer email, SMS, GitHub comments, or plain-text shares.

notifications.title: Alert Notifications

signalTypes.stateDescription: Ongoing system condition — stays until resolved

signalTypes.acknowledgedDescription: User has seen and dismissed this signal

email.configureFirst: Please configure all required email fields first
email.invalidSmtpPort: SMTP port must be a number between 1 and 65535.

notifications.tip: Configure notification channels here, then add them to specific alert rules in the Alert Rules editor. Each rule can have multiple notification channels with different configurations.

browser.title: Browser Notifications
browser.testBody: If you see this, browser notifications are working!
browser.enableInstructions: Make sure notifications are enabled: System Settings → Notifications → Google Chrome → Allow Notifications
browser.enableInstructionsChrome: Make sure notifications are enabled: System Settings → Notifications → Google Chrome → Allow Notifications.
browser.enableInstructionsFirefox: Make sure notifications are enabled: System Settings → Notifications → Firefox → Allow Notifications, and check Firefox's site permissions for this page.
browser.enableInstructionsSafari: Make sure notifications are enabled: System Settings → Notifications → Safari → Allow Notifications, and Safari → Settings → Websites → Notifications.
browser.enableInstructionsEdge: Make sure notifications are enabled: System Settings → Notifications → Microsoft Edge → Allow Notifications.
browser.enableInstructionsGeneric: Make sure notifications are enabled in your operating system's notification settings for this browser, and in the browser's site permissions for this page.
browser.dndHint: Also check that Do Not Disturb / Focus mode is off — the OS suppresses notifications while it's on.
browser.blocked: Browser notifications are blocked. Enable them in your browser's site settings for this page, then reload.
browser.quietHoursDesc: Suppress desktop notifications during a recurring daily window.

pagerduty.title: PagerDuty
pagerduty.routingKeyHint: Find this under Services > Integrations > Events API v2 in PagerDuty
pagerduty.testSuccess: Test notification sent and resolved successfully

opsgenie.apiKeyHint: Find this under Settings > API key management in OpsGenie
opsgenie.testSuccess: Test alert created and closed successfully

persistence.title: Deploy Persistence
persistence.subtitle: Store workloads, groups, and deployments as Kubernetes CRs
persistence.enableDesc: Store Deploy dashboard state as Custom Resources
persistence.failoverDesc: Failover to secondary cluster if primary is unavailable
persistence.namespaceHint: Namespace where console CRs will be stored
persistence.kubectlHint: When enabled, you can use kubectl to manage workloads, groups, and deployments. Changes made via kubectl will automatically sync to the UI.

localClusters.title: Local Clusters
localClusters.subtitle: Create and manage local Kubernetes clusters
localClusters.connectAgent: Connect the local agent to manage local clusters
localClusters.agentDesc: The local agent can detect and manage kind, k3d, and minikube clusters on your machine.
localClusters.installTools: Install one of these tools to create local clusters:
localClusters.noClusters: No local clusters found. Create one above to get started.
localClusters.deleteConfirm: Delete cluster "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
localClusters.vclusterDesc: Virtual clusters running inside host Kubernetes clusters
localClusters.vclusterInstallDesc: Install the vCluster CLI to create and manage virtual Kubernetes clusters
localClusters.vclusterDeleteConfirm: Delete virtual cluster "{{name}}" in namespace "{{namespace}}"? This cannot be undone.
localClusters.kubevirtDesc: Run virtual machines on Kubernetes clusters
localClusters.kubevirtNoClusters: No connected clusters. Connect a cluster to check for KubeVirt.
localClusters.kubevirtInstallHint: KubeVirt lets you run virtual machines alongside containers on your Kubernetes clusters. Use the guided mission to install it.

backup.title: Backup & Sync
backup.subtitle: Settings persisted to disk — survives cache clears and upgrades
backup.importFailed: Failed to import settings. Check that the file is a valid backup.
backup.securityNote: API keys, tokens, and credentials are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest. Non-sensitive preferences (theme, AI mode) are stored in plaintext for easy reading. Exported backups retain encryption — they can only be imported on machines with the same encryption key.

widget.title: Desktop Widget
widget.subtitle: Monitor clusters from your desktop
widget.browserWidgetDesc: Open in any browser. Use Safari PiP or Chrome Always-on-Top.
widget.ubersichtWidgetDesc: Native macOS desktop widget. Draggable and always visible.
widget.chromeHint: Chrome/Edge: Install an 'Always on Top' extension, then pin the widget tab.
widget.safariHint: Safari: Right-click the tab → 'Enter Picture in Picture' for a floating widget.
widget.step3: Ensure kc-agent is running on port 8585
widget.previewDesc: Draggable · Click stats to open console · Auto-refreshes every 30s
widget.selectWidget: Select a widget type above to see installation options

updates.title: System Updates
updates.subtitle: Manage updates for the console and Marketplace components
updates.devVersion: Running development version. Set {{envVar}} to test update detection.
updates.webConsoleDesc: Refresh your browser to load the latest version.
updates.localAgentDesc: Update via Homebrew to get the latest agent features.
updates.clusterDeploymentDesc: Update your Helm release to the latest chart version.
updates.devSourceDesc: Pull the latest changes, rebuild the frontend, and update the local agent.
updates.devMakeUpdateDesc: Pulls latest code, builds frontend and Go binaries, then restarts all processes.
updates.devCodingAgentDesc: AI coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf can pull, build, and restart the console for you automatically.
updates.developerDesc: Auto-pull, build, and restart when main changes
updates.uncommittedAutoStash: Local changes detected — they will be automatically stashed during updates and restored after
updates.agentRequiredDesc: Start kc-agent to enable automatic updates
updates.helmDisabled: Auto-update not available for Helm installs
updates.helmDisabledDesc: Use 'helm upgrade' to update your cluster deployment
updates.helmSelfUpgradeHint: Re-enable in-console upgrades: helm upgrade --set selfUpgrade.enabled=true
updates.helmSelfUpgradeDesc: Self-upgrade is enabled — you can upgrade directly from this page
updates.helmUpgradeWarning: This will restart the console pod. All users will briefly lose connection.
updates.helmRestartingDesc: Waiting for the new version to come online ({{seconds}}s elapsed)
updates.doNotNavigate: Please do not navigate away while the update is in progress.
updates.cancelHint: Cancels at the next step boundary — best-effort
updates.cancelUnavailable: Too late to cancel — restart already in progress
updates.cancelledHint: Any partial changes have been rolled back. You can retry from the Update Now button.
updates.allPrereqsMet: All prerequisites met — update notifications active
updates.prereqsMissing: {{count}} prerequisite(s) not met — some features unavailable
updates.errorHint: Try clicking "Check Now" to retry, or verify that kc-agent is running and network is available.
updates.scopeSystemDesc: Checks for new console releases on the selected Update Channel and lets you apply them. For self-hosted installs with kc-agent, enabling Automatic Updates will pull and apply new versions in the background. Without kc-agent, you will see a notification with manual upgrade instructions (Helm command, brew command, or git pull).
updates.scopeReloadDesc: After a new version is deployed (whether via auto-update or manual upgrade), the browser detects the updated build and shows a banner prompting you to reload the page. This happens automatically and has no settings on this page.
updates.scopeCardDataDesc: Dashboard cards fetch live data (cluster health, pod status, metrics) on their own schedules, independent of console version updates. Card refresh intervals are not controlled by these settings.
updates.scopeMarketplaceDesc: Cards, themes, and integrations installed from the Marketplace are updated independently by their publishers. These items are not covered by the console version update channel above. Check the Marketplace page for available updates to installed components, or enable per-item auto-update from each item's settings.

theme.title: Appearance
theme.subtitle: Choose your theme - inspired by oh-my-zsh
theme.removeThemeMessage: This will uninstall the theme and remove it from your list. This action cannot be undone.

profile.coinsUpdateHint: Coins update periodically with the leaderboard (approximately every 4 hours), not in real-time.

developerSetup.title: Developer Setup
developerSetup.description: Run the console from source with GitHub OAuth
developerSetup.oauthStep1: Create a new OAuth app at
developerSetup.oauthStep3: Click "Register application", then copy the Client ID and generate a Client Secret.
developerSetup.envFile: Create a .env file in the project root:
developerSetup.startDevDesc: Compiles backend + agent from source, starts Vite dev server with hot reload.
developerSetup.updateChannelDesc: Dev installs can track the main branch for automatic updates via commit SHA.
developerSetup.updateChannelNote: Checks for updates every 5 minutes and auto-rebuilds from source.

gpu.noGpuNodesDetected: No GPU nodes detected across your clusters

gitops.configureHint: Configure ArgoCD or Flux to see sync status
gitops.title: GitOps
gitops.subtitle: GitOps drift detection and sync status
gitops.dashboardDescription: Add cards to monitor ArgoCD applications, Helm releases, and GitOps sync status.
gitops.integrationDescription: GitOps integration detects drift between your Git repository and live cluster state using kubectl diff. Connect ArgoCD or Flux for enhanced sync capabilities.

welcome.subtitle: Up and running in two steps
welcome.step1Title: Download and run the install script
welcome.step1Desc: One command — downloads binaries, starts the console and kc-agent, and opens your browser. No Go, Node.js, or build tools required.
welcome.step2Desc: Once the script finishes, open http://localhost:8080 in your browser. Your clusters are auto-detected from ~/.kube/config.

reset.replaceAllDesc: Remove all current cards and replace them with the default set. This will remove any customizations.

feedback.title: Title
feedback.loginExplanation: Please login with GitHub to submit feedback, request updates, or close requests.
feedback.loginDemoExplanation: This is a public demo — GitHub login requires your own KubeStellar Console instance. Install locally to submit feedback and unlock all features.
feedback.demoDataBanner: Demo mode: the items below are sample data, not your submissions. Install your own console or log in via GitHub OAuth to see real feature requests.
feedback.oauthExplanation: To submit feedback and earn coins through the console, you need a GitHub OAuth app configured for your installation.
feedback.oauthStep1: Set up a GitHub OAuth app for your console (~2 minutes)
feedback.oauthStep2: Submit bugs and feature requests directly from the console
feedback.oauthStep3: Earn coins for each submission — our AI works on fixes automatically
feedback.githubIssuesInfo: You can open issues and requests directly on the kubestellar/console GitHub repo. Coins earned from GitHub issues will appear in your console once you configure OAuth.
feedback.alreadySetUp: Already have OAuth configured? Login with GitHub →
feedback.submitFailed: Unable to submit — GitHub login may be required.
feedback.submitFailedGuidance: You can open issues directly on GitHub. Coins will be accumulated and available in your console once you install a GitHub OAuth app.
feedback.submitInfo: Your request will be reviewed by a maintainer before being analyzed by our AI. Once approved, the AI will attempt to create a fix automatically and you'll receive a notification when ready.
feedback.loginBannerDemo: Login with GitHub to submit feedback and manage requests
feedback.loginBannerLocal: Set up GitHub OAuth to submit feedback, earn coins, and track requests
feedback.authGateBodyDemo: This is a public demo — GitHub login requires your own KubeStellar Console instance. Log in or install locally before filling out the form so your draft is not lost.
feedback.authGateBodyLocal: Set up a GitHub OAuth app (or log in if already configured) before filling out the form so your draft is not lost.
feedback.reportBugOrFeature: Report a bug or request a feature

nps.title: How's your experience with KubeStellar Console?
nps.feedbackNeutral: What would make it even better?
nps.thankYouDetail: Your input helps us improve KubeStellar Console.
nps.submitError: Couldn't send your feedback. Please try again.

argoApp.triggerSyncDesc: Reconcile the live cluster state with the desired state in Git.

argoApp.declarativeRestartDesc: To trigger a rolling restart declaratively, update the
argoApp.declarativeRestartDescSuffix: annotation in your deployment manifest and commit to Git. Argo CD will detect the change and apply it to the cluster.
argoApp.restartSnippetComment: # In your Git repository — update this annotation to trigger a restart
argoApp.afterCommitHint: After committing, either wait for Argo CD auto-sync or click
argoApp.benefit1: All changes are version-controlled and auditable via Git history.
argoApp.benefit2: Rollouts, restarts, and configuration changes are triggered by Git commits — not manual commands.
argoApp.benefit3: Works across multi-cluster workloads managed by KubeStellar and Argo CD.
argoApp.benefit4: Enterprise-grade compliance — no imperative commands scattered across clusters.
argoApp.syncSuccessMessage: Sync triggered for application "{{appName}}" in namespace "{{namespace}}". Argo CD will reconcile the desired state from Git.

acmmIntro.title: Welcome to the AI Codebase Maturity Model
acmmIntro.description: Score any GitHub repo on a 6-level framework for AI-assisted engineering
acmmIntro.whatIsBody: A 6-level framework scoring how ready a repo is for AI-assisted engineering. It detects concrete signals — instruction files, measurement workflows, feedback loops, gating policies — from L1 (ad-hoc completions) to L6 (fully autonomous). No source code is uploaded; only public file paths are read.
acmmIntro.levelL1Desc: AI suggests completions, no persistent rules
acmmIntro.levelL2Desc: Judgment encoded in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / Copilot instructions
acmmIntro.levelL3Desc: Rules mechanically enforced; metrics instrument the loop
acmmIntro.levelL4Desc: Metrics feed back into instructions; workflows are structured
acmmIntro.levelL5Desc: System detects + proposes — humans still approve
acmmIntro.levelL6Desc: System acts — generates issues, merges PRs, rolls back; humans set policy
acmmIntro.levelsNote: L5 vs L6: at L5 the system proposes and humans approve. At L6 the system acts and humans audit after the fact.
acmmIntro.learningBody: Each level maps to a skill your team acquires. Progressing from L1 to L6 teaches teams to encode judgment, measure outcomes, and close feedback loops — building lasting AI-development fluency.
acmmIntro.practiceBody: ACMM isn't a one-time audit. It tracks the progression from ad-hoc AI usage to structured, repeatable workflows. The criteria make best practices visible so teams can adopt them incrementally.
acmmIntro.traceabilityBody: Every criterion links back to its source framework, and every detected signal is tied to a concrete file or workflow in your repo. This citation trail lets you trace any score to evidence and any feedback loop to its origin.
acmmIntro.sourcesBody: The ACMM score draws on criteria from four open-source plugin registries. Each criterion is tagged with its source so you can trace any score back to the framework that defined it.
acmmIntro.sourceACMM: the core 6-level maturity model (L1-L6 levels + prerequisite checks)
acmmIntro.sourceFullsend: adds criteria for deployment readiness and autonomy (test coverage, CI/CD pipelines, auto-merge policies)
acmmIntro.sourceAEF: adds governance criteria for agentic workflows (task traceability, structural gates)
acmmIntro.sourceReflect: adds self-tuning criteria for agent learning loops (correction capture, CLAUDE.md auto-sync)
acmmIntro.actionScan: Type any {{ownerRepo}} in the picker to scan it.
acmmIntro.actionRole: See your role and the next transition trigger on the Your Role card.
acmmIntro.actionInventory: Browse detected vs missing criteria on the Feedback Loops Inventory card.
acmmIntro.actionAgent: Click Ask agent for help on any missing criterion to launch an agent that adds it.
acmmIntro.actionBadge: Generate a shields.io badge for your README — it updates as your score changes.
